sources.name: Grants.gov (Simpler Grants)
sources.notes: RESTful API; key obtained from account settings on simpler.grants.gov

sources.name: SAM.gov – Contract & Grant Opportunities
sources.notes: Public access: 10 req/day; registered entities: 1,000/day

sources.name: NIH RePORTER
sources.notes: POST-only endpoint (returns 405 on GET). Send JSON body with criteria object. Limit ~1 req/sec; v2 API active. API docs at api.reporter.nih.gov/?urls.primaryName=V2.0. Federal Reporter also at api.federalreporter.nih.gov

sources.name: NSF Award Search
sources.notes: Public API; no key needed. Also see NSF Developer Resources at nsf.gov/digital/developer

sources.name: SBIR.gov – Small Business Innovation Research
sources.notes: Topic API + Award API; bulk downloads in XLS/JSON/XML. Award data 290 MB full file

sources.name: USASpending.gov
sources.notes: Comprehensive open API; free; no key needed

sources.name: Federal Audit Clearinghouse (GSA)
sources.notes: Launched Oct 2023 by GSA, replacing old facdissem.census.gov. Open API; relates to grant compliance not opportunity discovery

sources.name: ARPA-H – Open Funding Opportunities
sources.notes: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health (ARPA-H); ISOs, programs, SBIR/STTR, and initiative opportunities listed on rolling basis

sources.name: DARPA – R&D Opportunities (BAAs)
sources.notes: Primary vehicle is Broad Agency Announcements (BAAs); posted to SAM.gov and DARPA site. Primarily contracts/cooperative agreements/OTs rather than traditional grants. BAOs accepted on rolling basis.

sources.name: PCORI – Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute
sources.notes: Independent non-profit authorized by Congress; funds CER and D&I research. Three cycles per year; up to $120M per Broad Pragmatic Studies PFA cycle. No API; scraping required.

sources.name: USDA NIFA – National Institute of Food and Agriculture
sources.notes: Applications via Grants.gov. Funds competitive, capacity, and noncompetitive grants for research, education, and extension. NIFA Grant Funding Dashboard available at nifa.usda.gov.

sources.name: DOE Office of Science – PAMS Grant Portal
sources.notes: Uses PAMS (Portfolio Analysis and Management System) for applications; FOAs posted on Grants.gov and science.osti.gov. Award search at pamspublic.science.energy.gov.

sources.name: NEH – National Endowment for the Humanities
sources.notes: Funded Projects Query Form at apps.neh.gov/publicquery provides abstracts back to 1980. Grants posted on Grants.gov. No dedicated REST API.

sources.name: IMLS – Institute of Museum and Library Services
sources.notes: Award abstracts back to 1997 at imls.gov/grants. Applications via Grants.gov. Free public API for award data: api.imls.gov.

sources.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
sources.notes: No public API. Download the spreadsheet from the committed grants page. Also accessible via Candid/ProPublica

sources.name: Wellcome Trust
sources.notes: No direct Wellcome API. Data accessible via 360Giving GrantNav API & Datastore. 360Giving also publishes bulk data

sources.name: Wellcome Open Research (publications/grants)
sources.notes: F1000-based developer API for article/grant data

sources.name: Ford Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Also available via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990 data

sources.name: MacArthur Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Available via Candid FDO

sources.name: Open Society Foundations
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Also accessible via Candid

sources.name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (RWJF)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Also in Candid FDO

sources.name: Rockefeller Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Accessible via Candid & ProPublica

sources.name: Bloomberg Philanthropies
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Some data via ProPublica 990

sources.name: Simons Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Alfred P. Sloan Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Available via Candid FDO

sources.name: Open Philanthropy (formerly OpenPhil)
sources.notes: No API; structured HTML — relatively easy to scrape

sources.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Andrew W. Mellon Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Kresge Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Lumina Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. $11.4B endowment; distributed $401.8M in 2024 across 952 grants. Focus areas: Environmental Conservation, Science, Bay Area. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. $8.2B endowment; focuses on children, families, communities, and racial equity. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: William & Flora Hewlett Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. Awarded >$450M in grants. Programs include education, global development & population, performing arts, environment, effective philanthropy. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Pew Charitable Trusts
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. $1.35B+ in assets. Operates as both a foundation and public charity. Does not typically accept unsolicited applications; proactive grantmaking model. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Carnegie Corporation of New York
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. $4.1B endowment. Focus: education, democracy, peace and security. Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI)
sources.notes: One of the largest private biomedical research funders in the US; $27.1B endowment. Announced $245M for AI-driven life sciences projects. Investigator Program for established researchers; Science Education programs open competitively. No API; scraping required. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Burroughs Wellcome Fund
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. Focuses on early-career biomedical scientists and underfunded areas of science. Partners with HHMI on Medical Research Fellows Program. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Children's Investment Fund Foundation (CIFF)
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. Disbursed $468M in 2021. Offices in London, Nairobi, Addis Ababa, Beijing, New Delhi. Does not solicit unsolicited proposals; identifies grantees through own research. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Mastercard Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. One of the world's largest foundations; headquartered in Toronto. Focuses on young people in Africa and Indigenous youth in Canada. Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: EU Funding & Tenders Portal (Horizon, LIFE, etc.)
sources.notes: Canonical EC SEDIA API source (apiKey=SEDIA). Fetches all open/forthcoming calls across Horizon, ERC, EIC, LIFE, Digital Europe and more. All other eu_funding sources are paused to avoid duplicates.

sources.name: Horizon Europe / CORDIS
sources.notes: Redundant — covered by EU Funding & Tenders Portal (Horizon, LIFE, etc.) via the same SEDIA API. Paused to prevent duplicate grants.

sources.name: EU Funding & Grants Portal (eufundingportal.eu – aggregator)
sources.notes: Third-party aggregator site — not the EC API. Changed source_type to ai_scraper (Playwright). Paused until a proper scraper config is written for this site.

sources.name: European Research Council (ERC)
sources.notes: ERC calls accessible through the main EU Funding & Tenders Portal API

sources.name: European Innovation Council (EIC)
sources.notes: Redundant — EIC calls (EIC-ACCELERATOR, EIC-PATHFINDER, etc.) are already returned by the canonical EU Funding & Tenders Portal source via the SEDIA API. Paused to prevent duplicates.

sources.name: European Structural & Investment Funds (ESIF / Cohesion)
sources.notes: ESIF/Cohesion uses a Socrata-based platform — different from the EC SEDIA API. Paused until a dedicated scraper is implemented for cohesiondata.ec.europa.eu.

sources.name: EU Funding & Tenders – Horizon Programmes
sources.notes: Redundant — Horizon programmes are fully covered by the canonical EU Funding & Tenders Portal source. Paused to prevent duplicates.

sources.name: DFG – Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (German Research Foundation)
sources.notes: GEPRIS database (gepris.dfg.de) covers all DFG-funded projects since early 2000s with full-text search. REST API available for GEPRIS. Funding programmes page lists all current calls. Major bilateral agreements with CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC and others.

sources.name: Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF)
sources.notes: Applications via the SNSF Portal (portal.snf.ch). P3 database for funded projects (p3.snf.ch). Active 2026 joint call with NHMRC Australia for AI in healthcare research. Also partners with Tri-Agency Canada and DFG Germany.

sources.name: UKRI Gateway to Research (GtR)
sources.notes: Free, open API; data available via 3 API endpoints. Also bulk CSV download

sources.name: UKRI Funding Opportunities
sources.notes: No API for live calls; scraping required. Awarded grants accessible via GtR API

sources.name: FCDO DevTracker (UK Foreign Aid)
sources.notes: Built on IATI OIPA API; data updated monthly; JSON download per programme

sources.name: UK International Development Funding (GOV.UK)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Published under IATI standard

sources.name: IATI Datastore / D-Portal
sources.notes: REST API returning XML/JSON/CSV; d-portal.org is the browser-based explorer

sources.name: d-portal (IATI visualizer)
sources.notes: No separate API; access data via IATI Datastore API

sources.name: AidData (College of William & Mary)
sources.notes: No live REST API; bulk dataset downloads in CSV/Stata format

sources.name: World Bank Open Data / Projects
sources.notes: Comprehensive free API; Indicators API, Projects API, Data Catalog API all documented

sources.name: Asian Development Bank (ADB) Tenders & Grants
sources.notes: No formal public API; RSS feeds available; scraping or OpenOpps.com aggregator

sources.name: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB/IADB)
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required; some data via AidData

sources.name: African Development Bank (AfDB) Tenders
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required; data via IATI if published

sources.name: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD – ECEPP)
sources.notes: No public API; ECEPP portal requires registration; scraping possible from public notices

sources.name: European Investment Bank (EIB)
sources.notes: Open data portal with bulk downloads; no REST API but structured datasets available

sources.name: UNDP Funding Windows Portal
sources.notes: No API; scraping required; UNDP publishes some IATI data

sources.name: UN Global Marketplace (UNGM) – Procurement
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required

sources.name: UNICEF Supply Division / Procurement
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: WHO Procurement / Calls
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: USAID (foreign aid grants/contracts)
sources.notes: Opportunities accessible via SAM.gov API and Grants.gov API. USAID DEC now archived

sources.name: Global Fund to Fight AIDS, TB & Malaria
sources.notes: No API; limited structured data; scraping required

sources.name: Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: DevelopmentAid.org
sources.notes: Paid API for partners; automates retrieval of grants, tenders, jobs. Contact DevelopmentAid for access

sources.name: Candid Foundation Directory Online (FDO)
sources.notes: Grants API with /funders and /recipients endpoints. Subscription-based

sources.name: Candid / 990 Finder (Nonprofit data)
sources.notes: Multiple APIs: Grants, Essentials, Profiles. Tiered pricing

sources.name: Instrumentl
sources.notes: No public API; closed SaaS platform. Subscription required for access

sources.name: GrantWatch
sources.notes: No API; $249/yr subscription; scraping possible but ToS may restrict

sources.name: Pivot-RP (ProQuest/Clarivate)
sources.notes: API used by universities for internal matching tools; requires institutional subscription

sources.name: OpenGrants.io
sources.notes: REST/JSON API; developer portal at developer.opengrants.io; invite-only access

sources.name: GrantStation
sources.notes: No public API; membership subscription required

sources.name: fundsforNGOs
sources.notes: No API; free website; scraping feasible

sources.name: Terra Viva Grants
sources.notes: No API; free listings; scraping feasible

sources.name: Inside Philanthropy (Grant Finder)
sources.notes: No API; subscription required; scraping restricted

sources.name: 360Giving GrantNav (UK Philanthropic Grants)
sources.notes: Free HTTP/JSON API; bulk CSV/JSON download of full dataset; Datastore access for complex queries

sources.name: OpenAlex (research funding metadata)
sources.notes: Free open API; no key required for polite pool (<10 req/sec); CC0 data

sources.name: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer (IRS 990)
sources.notes: Free API; follows simple data terms of use; v2 API active

sources.name: eCivis (Government Grant Management)
sources.notes: API for enterprise clients; no public documentation

sources.name: OpenOpps.com (World Bank, ADB, IDB tenders)
sources.notes: Commercial API aggregating multilateral development bank procurement

sources.name: Google.org – Impact Challenges & Grants
sources.notes: No public API; monitor for open calls on google.org. Active Impact Challenges include AI for Science ($30M global call for health/life sciences and climate AI) and AI for Government Innovation ($1-3M per awardee). Digital Futures Fund >$35M committed globally. Scraping feasible.

sources.name: Microsoft AI for Good
sources.notes: No public API; programs include AI for Health, AI for Earth, AI for Humanitarian Action, AI for Cultural Heritage, AI for Accessibility. Grants and Azure credits awarded to nonprofits and researchers. Open calls vary. Also see Microsoft Philanthropies at microsoft.com/en-us/philanthropies.

sources.name: Bezos Earth Fund
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. $10B committed to climate and nature. AI for Climate and Nature Grand Challenge: up to $100M total; Phase II awardees receive up to $2M each. Active partnership with AI2050 and other climate AI initiatives. Monitor for open calls.

sources.name: Schmidt Sciences (formerly Schmidt Futures)
sources.notes: No public API; competitive RFPs at schmidtsciences.smapply.io; does not accept unsolicited proposals. $1.59B in assets; $228.7M in giving in FY2024. Active programs: AI2050 fellowships, carbon cycle research, HAVI institute. Rebranded from Schmidt Futures in 2024.

sources.name: Skoll Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; does not accept unsolicited proposals; selects grantees through internal process and community recommendations. Funds social entrepreneurs in health/pandemics, climate change, inclusive economies, effective governance, racial justice. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Grand Challenges (BMGF / USAID / Grand Challenges Canada)
sources.notes: No API; searchable database on website; scraping feasible

sources.name: Horizon Europe NCP Portal
sources.notes: No API; informational portal; links back to EU F&T Portal

sources.name: EMDESK (Horizon Europe project management)
sources.notes: No API for grant discovery; project management SaaS only

sources.name: Wellcome Leap (health moonshots)
sources.notes: No API; small number of active calls; monitor directly

sources.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation – Grand Challenges Explorations
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: UK Research & Innovation (UKRI) – Active Opportunities
sources.notes: No API for live calls; awarded project data accessible via GtR API

sources.name: European Commission Open Data Portal
sources.notes: DCAT-AP compliant; SPARQL endpoint and REST API available

sources.name: Innovate UK – Smart Grants & Competitions
sources.notes: No API; scraping required for open competitions; awarded data via GtR

sources.name: EUREKA Network (collaborative R&D)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Newton Fund / British Council
sources.notes: No API; scraping required

sources.name: Climate & Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) Grants
sources.notes: No API; small volume of calls; monitor directly

sources.name: GEF (Global Environment Facility) Projects
sources.notes: Open data portal with API; project browser and bulk data downloads

sources.name: Green Climate Fund (GCF)
sources.notes: Open data portal; project data downloadable; REST API available

sources.name: Adaptation Fund
sources.notes: No formal API; project list scrapeable

sources.name: CGIAR Research Programs (agriculture)
sources.notes: No API; limited structured grant data publicly available

sources.name: FundsforNGOs Premium / Grants Alert
sources.notes: No API; subscription email service; free and paid tiers

sources.name: CIHR – Canadian Institutes of Health Research
sources.notes: Canada's primary health research funder. Funding Decisions Database at webapps.cihr-irsc.gc.ca. Applies via ResearchNet (researchnet-recherchenet.ca). Part of Canadian Tri-Agency (CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC). Interagency calendar at canada.ca for all Canadian federal programs.

sources.name: NSERC – Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada
sources.notes: Canada's federal funding agency for natural sciences and engineering research. Applications via Research Portal (portal-portail.nserc-crsng.gc.ca). Part of Tri-Agency with CIHR and SSHRC. Active joint calls with DFG, ANR, and other international partners. No public REST API for opportunity discovery.

sources.name: IDRC – International Development Research Centre (Canada)
sources.notes: Canadian Crown corporation funding applied research in developing countries. Partners with NRF South Africa, African Academy of Sciences, and others. Active open calls for proposals published on idrc-crdi.ca. Also participates in Science Granting Councils Initiative (SGCI). No public REST API.

sources.name: ARC – Australian Research Council
sources.notes: Australia's primary competitive research funder (excluding health/medical). Uses RMS (Research Management System) for grant submission. New opportunities under NCGP (National Competitive Grants Program) schemes announced regularly. GrantConnect (grants.gov.au) lists Australian government grants. No public REST API.

sources.name: NHMRC – National Health and Medical Research Council (Australia)
sources.notes: Australia's primary health and medical research funder. Applications via Sapphire portal. Active 2026 joint call with Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) for AI in healthcare research (deadline May 28, 2026). Also see GrantConnect (grants.gov.au) for all Australian government grants. No public REST API.

sources.name: NRF – National Research Foundation (South Africa)
sources.notes: South Africa's primary research funding agency, supported by the Department of Science and Innovation (DSI). Partners with IDRC Canada, African Academy of Sciences, and others through the Science Granting Councils Initiative (SGCI). No public REST API; scraping required.

sources.name: ResearchProfessional (Clarivate)
sources.notes: Institutional API available; subscription required

sources.name: Dimensions (Digital Science) – Grants
sources.notes: DSL query language API; free tier for non-commercial; institutional subscription for full access

sources.name: CrossRef Grant Metadata
sources.notes: Free REST API; funder data underpins OpenAlex and Dimensions grant metadata

sources.name: Europe PubMed Central (Europe PMC) – Grants
sources.notes: REST API with grant search; supports funders who mandate open access

sources.name: Semantic Scholar (AI2) – Funding metadata
sources.notes: Free API key; paper funding data extracted by AI; less complete than Dimensions

sources.name: Pro Helvetia – Swiss Arts Council
sources.notes: Swiss federal foundation supporting artists and cultural projects; offers grants, residencies, and co-production support internationally

sources.name: NEA – National Endowment for the Arts
sources.notes: Only federal arts funder serving all 50 states. Uses its own Applicant Portal (AP) at apps.arts.gov for some categories and Grants.gov for others. Grant search at grantsearch.nea.gov. FY 2026 Grants for Arts Projects active; Challenge America canceled for FY2026.

sources.name: DOE ARPA-E – Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (eXCHANGE)
sources.notes: ARPA-E eXCHANGE is the mandatory submission portal for all ARPA-E Notices of Intent, Concept Papers, Full Applications, and Replies. ~$400M annual budget; awards $500K–$10M over 2–3 years. DARPA-inspired high-risk/high-reward model. Also see ARPA-E homepage at arpa-e.energy.gov.

sources.name: NASA – NSPIRES (Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Science)
sources.notes: NASA's official research solicitation and proposal management system. Organizations must register in SAM.gov before registering in NSPIRES. ROSES-26 (Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Science) releases July 2026. Also see NASA grant opportunities at nasa.gov/grants. Awarded project data also on research.gov.

sources.name: DOD – ONR / ARO / AFOSR (Defense Research BAAs)
sources.notes: Three main DoD basic research offices: Office of Naval Research (onr.navy.mil), Army Research Office (arl.devcom.army.mil), Air Force Office of Scientific Research (afrl.af.mil/afosr). All BAAs submitted via Grants.gov/SAM.gov. Major annual programs include DURIP (research instrumentation) and MURI (multidisciplinary university research). FY2026 MURI ~$170M total across ONR, ARO, AFOSR.

sources.name: AHRQ – Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$380M annual budget; smallest HHS research agency. Funds health services research, patient safety, and healthcare quality improvement (not biomedical research). Notice of Funding Opportunities at ahrq.gov/funding/fund-opps. Uses eRA Commons for NIH-adjacent grants; RFAs issued for priority research areas. Also see GOLD database for funded grants.

sources.name: CDC – Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Grants
sources.notes: CDC Office of Financial Resources manages eligibility and grant opportunities. ~$8–9B in grants annually. Opportunities posted on Grants.gov. Award tracking via HHS TAGGS (taggs.hhs.gov). Uses eRA Commons for grant proposals. Key programs: PPHF, public health infrastructure, prevention research, global health security.

sources.name: HRSA – Health Resources and Services Administration
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$12.2B in FY2024 grants. Focus: community health centers, rural health, health workforce programs. Applications via Grants.gov; award data via HHS TAGGS. Key programs: FQHC, Rural Health Outreach, Health Center Program, National Health Service Corps.

sources.name: SAMHSA – Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$6–7B annual grants. SAMHSA Grants Dashboard (samhsa.gov/grants/grants-dashboard) lists open, current, and past grants from 2014 to present including NOFO forecasts. FY2026 Cooperative Agreement for 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline: $231.5M award ceiling from $1B total program. Applications via Grants.gov.

sources.name: ANR – Agence Nationale de la Recherche (France)
sources.notes: France's national research funding agency under Ministry of Higher Education and Research. Funds basic and applied research across all disciplines. Active bilateral programs with NSERC, DFG, NWO and others. Part of FLAG-ERA consortium for Future and Emerging Technologies. Applications via ANR's online portal. Member of Open Research Europe consortium.

sources.name: NWO – Dutch Research Council (Netherlands)
sources.notes: National research council of the Netherlands; funds 5000+ scientists annually. Apply directly when a call is published on nwo.nl. Includes Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) programs. Member of Open Research Europe and FLAG-ERA consortia. Also accessible via TRIMIS (EC transport research). No bulk API; scraping required.

sources.name: Swedish Research Council (Vetenskapsrådet)
sources.notes: Sweden's largest governmental research funding body; distributes funds for basic research of the highest scientific quality in all fields. Participates in NordForsk Nordic cooperation. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. Applications via Swedish Research Council's online application system. No public REST API.

sources.name: Research Council of Norway (Norges Forskningsråd)
sources.notes: Norway's government research policy advisor and primary funding body; allocates NOK 10Bn annually for research and innovation. Participates in NordForsk Nordic cooperation. Applications via Research Council of Norway's portal (prosjektbanken.forskningsradet.no for open project data). No public bulk API.

sources.name: NordForsk – Nordic Research and Innovation Funding
sources.notes: Organisation under Nordic Council of Ministers; provides funding for Nordic research cooperation. Funding partners include the research councils of Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden. Applications via NordForsk Application Portal (online). 2026 active call: Nordic exploratory networks in humanities/social sciences (NOK 33M total). No public REST API.

sources.name: FWF – Austrian Science Fund
sources.notes: Austria's primary research funding agency; DFG partner organisation in Austria. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. Applications via FWF's online portal (elane.fwf.ac.at). Open data on funded projects at fwf.ac.at/en/research-funding/fwf-funded-projects. No public REST API.

sources.name: FCT – Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia (Portugal)
sources.notes: Portugal's main funding agency for research and scientific-technological development. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. Applications via MyFCT portal. Funded projects accessible via FCT's open data portal. No public REST API.

sources.name: Academy of Finland / Research Council of Finland
sources.notes: Finland's primary research funding body; participates in NordForsk Nordic cooperation. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. Applications via Academy of Finland's online service (at.aka.fi). Funded projects accessible via open data portal. No public REST API.

sources.name: Novo Nordisk Foundation
sources.notes: As of Feb 2026, the wealthiest charitable foundation in the world (~$220B net worth). Awarded US$1.83B in grants in 2024. Applications via 'NORMA' online application system. Active 2026 programs include NNF Laureate Research Grant and NERD Programme (New Exploratory Research and Discovery). Major 2026 grants include €736M to BioInnovation Institute and DKK 5.5B to elevate Danish/European innovation. Also co-funds with Lundbeck Foundation.

sources.name: Research Portal Denmark (Forskningsportal.dk)
sources.notes: Consolidated Danish grant database aggregating data from Carlsberg Foundation, Independent Research Fund Denmark (DFF), Lundbeck Foundation, Novo Nordisk Foundation, VELUX FOUNDATION, and Villum Foundation. Central search interface for Danish philanthropic and research grants. No public REST API; scraping feasible.

sources.name: VolkswagenStiftung (Volkswagen Foundation)
sources.notes: Major German private science foundation; supports humanities, social sciences, science, and technology. Applications via the Foundation's Funding Platform (online). Active 2026 programs: Lichtenberg Endowed Professorships (deadline Jun 20, 2026), Opus Magnum Scholarship (deadline Nov 18, 2026), Challenges for Europe. Granted €10.6M for 8 'Challenges for Europe' projects. No public REST API.

sources.name: Robert Bosch Stiftung (Robert Bosch Foundation)
sources.notes: One of the major foundations in Europe, associated with Robert Bosch GmbH. Supports education, health, social affairs, and civic engagement. Continuous application process via central online portal for project partners. Targets nonprofits and educational institutions. No public REST API; scraping required.

sources.name: JSPS – Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (KAKENHI)
sources.notes: Japan's leading research funding agency; KAKENHI (Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research) is Japan's primary competitive grant program. Categories include Scientific Research (A/B/C), Challenging Research, Early-Career Scientists. Applications via JSPS Electronic Application System (e-Rad) through affiliated institutions. Annual cycle: applications open July, close September. Active bilateral programs with NRF Korea, NRF South Africa, CIHR Canada, and others. KAKEN database at nii.ac.jp provides funded project data.

sources.name: NRF – National Research Foundation of Korea
sources.notes: South Korea's primary research funding body. Active bilateral programs with JSPS Japan (Korea-Japan Basic Scientific Cooperation Program, 2026 call active), NRF South Africa, and others. Application portal at ernd.nrf.re.kr. No public bulk API; scraping required.

sources.name: DBT – Department of Biotechnology (India)
sources.notes: India's federal biotechnology research funder under Ministry of Science and Technology. Funds biotech research, fellowships, and industry programs. Applications via DBT-BIO-CARe and other DBT portals. See also BIRAC (Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council) for industry grants. Also see DST (dstindia.gov.in) for broader science and technology grants.

sources.name: SSHRC – Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada
sources.notes: Canada's federal funding agency for social sciences and humanities. Part of Canadian Tri-Agency (CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC) with shared application via ResearchNet. Key 2026 programs: Connection Grants, Insight Grants, Insight Development Grants, Partnership Grants. Launched pilot Policy Innovation Partnership Grants program in 2026. Interagency program calendar at canada.ca. No public REST API.

sources.name: CFI – Canada Foundation for Innovation
sources.notes: Canadian Crown corporation investing in state-of-the-art facilities and equipment at universities, colleges, research hospitals, and nonprofit research institutions. In March 2026, Government of Canada invested >$552M via CFI for research infrastructure. CFI Innovation Fund (CFI-IF) is the major competitive program. Applications via CFI's online portal. No public REST API.

sources.name: David and Lucile Packard Foundation
sources.notes: Major US private foundation; grants database shows last 5 years of grantmaking (1,197+ grantees). Focus areas: Conservation and Science, Population and Reproductive Health, Children's Health and Development. Proactive grantmaking; does not generally accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Joyce Foundation
sources.notes: Chicago-based private foundation focusing on the Great Lakes region. Searchable grants database at joycefdn.org/grants-database. Focus: education, employment, environment, democracy, gun violence prevention, arts. Does not accept unsolicited proposals for most programs. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: John S. and James L. Knight Foundation
sources.notes: Focuses on informed and engaged communities through journalism, arts, and informed civic life. Searchable grants database at knightfoundation.org/grants. Also active in technology and AI for democracy. Invitation-only model for most programs; some open challenge competitions. Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Spencer Foundation
sources.notes: Invests in education research that cultivates learning and transforms lives. Funds large research grants, small research grants, and field-initiated research. Open LOI/application cycles published on website. In 2026 partners with William T. Grant, Annie E. Casey, and Doris Duke on $2.6M Institutional Challenge Grants. Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Ewing Marion Kauffman Foundation
sources.notes: Focus: equitable economic mobility through entrepreneurship and education. Research grant opportunities opening in summer 2026. Does not accept unsolicited proposals for most programs. Current grantees listed at kauffman.org/grants/current-grantees. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: William T. Grant Foundation
sources.notes: Focuses on reducing inequality in youth outcomes and improving use of research evidence in policy and practice. Active 2026 programs: William T. Grant Scholars ($425K for early-career scholars), Institutional Challenge Grant (up to $650K, partnering with Annie E. Casey, Bezos Family, Doris Duke, Spencer). Open LOI submissions. Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation
sources.notes: US foundation with ~$7.36B in assets (Dec 2023). Programs: safe water access, homelessness, foster care youth, Catholic sisters, substance use prevention. Annual Hilton Humanitarian Prize (2026 nominations open May 2025 – April 2026). Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Aga Khan Foundation
sources.notes: Part of the Aga Khan Development Network (AKDN). Active in health, education, rural development, and civil society. International Scholarship Programme open for 2026–2027 cycle. Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 open for mid-career Canadian professionals (deadline May 19, 2026). Works in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. No public REST API.

sources.name: Doris Duke Foundation
sources.notes: Formerly Doris Duke Charitable Foundation. Focus: arts, environmental conservation, medical research, and child well-being. Participates in 2026 Institutional Challenge Grants with William T. Grant, Annie E. Casey, and Spencer Foundations. Grant resources at dorisduke.org/grants. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Annie E. Casey Foundation
sources.notes: Major US foundation focused on vulnerable children, youth, and families. Proactive grantmaking; does not accept unsolicited proposals for most programs. Partners with William T. Grant, Spencer, Bezos Family, and Doris Duke on 2026 Institutional Challenge Grants. Grant info at aecf.org/grant-making. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Alzheimer's Association – International Research Grant Program
sources.notes: Funds research to advance understanding of Alzheimer's, identify treatments, and improve care. Open to US and international researchers. Multiple 2026 active calls: AARGA (LOI Feb 23, Application Apr 21), HSR-ADRD (LOI May 6, Application Jul 22), VCID-UMD Strategic Fund. Applications submitted via Grants.gov or foundation portal. Also accessible via Candid.

sources.name: American Cancer Society – Research Grants
sources.notes: Major US cancer research funder. Grant types include Research Scholar Grants, Postdoctoral Fellowships, and mission-specific grants. Electronic application deadlines June 1 and December 1 each year. 2026 application via ProposalCentral platform. Joint calls with Cancer Research UK (CRUK); US PI budget due June 11, 2026. Policies effective March 2026 updated grant terms.

sources.name: American Heart Association – Research Funding
sources.notes: Major US health research funder for cardiovascular and stroke research. Multiple active 2026 programs including Innovative Project Award and Predoctoral Fellowship. Applications submitted via ProposalCentral (research.americanheart.org). Also funds council awards and other recognitions via ProposalCentral.

sources.name: JDRF – Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation
sources.notes: International nonprofit dedicated to T1D research and cures. Funds basic research, clinical trials, and industry partnerships. Applications submitted via JDRF's online grant portal (jdrf.org/research). Does not accept unsolicited letters of inquiry for most programs; contact grants@jdrf.org. Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Cystic Fibrosis Foundation – Research Grants
sources.notes: Funds CF research through multiple grant types. Applications via awards.cff.org portal by 5pm ET. Two annual cycles (spring and fall); applicants may submit only one Research Grant application per year in 2026. Also accessible via ProposalCentral for some programs.

sources.name: ProposalCentral – Grant Submission Aggregator
sources.notes: E-grantmaking platform hosting grant opportunities for hundreds of organizations including American Heart Association, American Cancer Society, Cystic Fibrosis Foundation, American Association for Cancer Research, PhRMA, Huntington's Disease Society of America, and others. Lists all current open opportunities at proposalcentral.com/GrantOpportunities.asp. No formal public API but grant listings are scrapeable.

sources.name: ClimateWorks Foundation
sources.notes: Global platform for philanthropy to scale high-impact climate solutions. Since 2008, granted >$2B to 850+ grantees in 50 countries. Grants range $5K–$10M, most <$500K. Application by invitation only or selective calls for proposals—no open rolling applications. Leads Adaptation and Resilience Fund (with Laudes Foundation, Rockefeller, Howden, Quadrature Climate). No public REST API.

sources.name: European Climate Foundation
sources.notes: Major philanthropic initiative working with 700+ partners to catalyse climate action in Europe and globally. Funded by major international foundations. Does not have open grant calls; proactive partnership model with select grantees. Monitor europeanclimate.org for open RFPs and partner calls. No public REST API.

sources.name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
sources.notes: Boston-based foundation channeling AI and technology into positive social impact. In Dec 2024 announced $73.5M toward AI for Humanity supporting 144 organizations. Work spans digital health, climate change, IT, neuroscience, education, and empowering vulnerable communities. One of 10 founding funders of the Humanity AI initiative ($500M over 5 years). No public API; monitor for open RFPs.

sources.name: Mozilla Foundation – Grants
sources.notes: Funds organizations and individuals working on trustworthy AI, internet health, privacy, and open technology. Founding funder of Humanity AI initiative with Omidyar Network and Patrick J. McGovern Foundation. Active programs include Mozilla Technology Fund and Awards. Some open calls; primarily invitation-based. No public REST API.

sources.name: Omidyar Network
sources.notes: Impact investment and philanthropy firm by Pierre and Pam Omidyar. Does not accept unsolicited grant proposals. Grantmaking addresses role of technology in society, global and economic development, democracy, journalism, media. Co-founder of Humanity AI initiative ($500M). Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Leverhulme Trust
sources.notes: Major UK research funder supporting research and education across all subject areas except medical research. Emphasis on individuals and innovation. Online Grant Management System at grants.leverhulme.ac.uk for applications and award management. Outline applications paused; full applications to be accepted in new grants management system in autumn 2026. Grant listings at leverhulme.ac.uk/listings.

sources.name: Nuffield Foundation
sources.notes: UK foundation funding research, analysis, and student programmes to advance educational opportunity and social well-being across the UK. Also funds Nuffield Council on Bioethics and Nuffield Department of Medicine. Open application cycles published on website. No public REST API; scraping feasible.

sources.name: Joseph Rowntree Foundation
sources.notes: UK foundation working to unlock the UK from poverty. Funds research and programme partners. Also manages Joseph Rowntree Housing Trust and Joseph Rowntree Charitable Trust. Open calls published at jrf.org.uk/funding. No public REST API; scraping feasible.

sources.name: Esmée Fairbairn Foundation
sources.notes: UK foundation improving quality of life through arts, children and young people, environment, food, and social change. Open application process; apply via online portal on the website. Data accessible via 360Giving. No public REST API.

sources.name: Gatsby Charitable Foundation
sources.notes: UK charitable foundation active in plant science, neuroscience, education, Africa, public policy, and arts. Primarily proactive grantmaking; does not typically accept unsolicited applications. Some open calls via partner organizations. Data accessible via 360Giving. No public REST API.

sources.name: SPIN – Sponsored Programs Information Network (InfoEd)
sources.notes: Grant database published by InfoEd Global containing 8,000+ current funding opportunities including grants, fellowships, and support programs from 10,000+ sponsors (~40,000 opportunities on any given day). Updated almost daily. Provided via institutional subscriptions to InfoEd Electronic Research Administration (ERA) Suite. Used by Yale, Penn, UNC, and other major research universities.

sources.name: GrantForward
sources.notes: Subscription-based funding opportunity aggregator using specialized data-crawling technology. Database covers 30,000+ sponsors and thousands of grant opportunities across most disciplines. Every opportunity analyzed and verified by a team of specialists. Used by many research universities. Includes sponsor-level profiles (e.g., William T. Grant Foundation, HHMI). Formerly known as GrantFinder.
